title: [FIRRTL] width infer (leq) mismatch
body:
The following FIRRTL program

```python
circuit top_mod :
  module top_mod :
    output tmp118: UInt<1>
    tmp118 <= leq(UInt("b11001110001000110000"), UInt<8>("b10100001"))
```

Compiled with `firtool` produces this Verilog:

```verilog
// Standard header to adapt well known macros to our needs.

module top_mod(
  output tmp118);

  assign tmp118 = 1'h1;	// a_top_mod.fir:2:3, :4:15
endmodule
```

Compiled with `firrtl-1.5-SNAPSHOT` produces this Verilog:

```verilog
module top_mod(
  output  tmp118
);
  assign tmp118 = 1'h0;
endmodule
```

`Yosys 0.9+3911 (git sha1 4b31223e, clang 11.0.1 -fPIC -Os)` reports formal mismatch:

```
ERROR: Found 1 unproven $equiv cells in 'equiv_status -assert'.
```
